steps.title: Set up the equation
steps.content:
We need the number of games when total cost is \$76. Substitute into the function:
$$16 + 5x = 76$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Subtract 16 from both sides:
$$5x = 60$$
Divide both sides by 5:
$$x = 12$$
The variable $x$ counts games, so the solution means 12 games can be played for \$76. Each additional game adds \$5 after the initial \$16 fee, matching the linear cost model.
